data.task:
DOCUMENTATION ONLY (atom 0 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, governed by ADR-0036). Edit ONLY `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md`. Touch no code, tests, personas, ADRs, or any other file.

Goal: record the owner map for the Oscar↔Deb autonomous repair dialogue BEFORE its runtime is built (decision/owner-map-first), and reconcile the stale row that currently documents the within-run `deb-investigate` escalation (which this priority removes in atom 2).

Do TWO things:

1) ADD a new owner-map entry for the repair-dialogue contract. Match the doc's existing structure (add a row to the `## Owner Inventory` table near line 57, and/or a short dated addendum section consistent with the run_163/run_164 addenda style if a single table row cannot carry it). It must capture:
   - Source of truth: ADR-0036 (refines ADR-0016 + ADR-0013); fixes land via the EXISTING ADR-0016 repair authority + the ADR-0023 commit spine — no new commit lane.
   - Trigger: a daemon-resident, Oscar-initiated, Bob-free standing capability modeled on the existing idle Oz repair tool (`requestOzRepair`, `packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts` / `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`); runnable any time INCLUDING after Oscar has wrapped; NOT a within-`runRun` directive kind.
   - Handshake artifacts: the propose→evaluate→direct flow — Oscar files a repair request (named problem + evidence); Deb either applies an easy in-scope fix (gate-committed as a distinct `deb-repair`) OR hands a proposal back to Oscar; Oscar evaluates and directs; evidence recorded.
   - Founder-escalation tier: genuinely risky / hard-to-reverse items escalate one tier further to the founder (ADR-0016 §4 lightest-home, extended).
   - Invariants: Oscar↔Deb only (Deb never directs Bob); no second orchestration lane and no second commit lane; never enters the build directive loop; never rescues a formally failed run; does NOT replace Oscar's per-atom verify gate over Bob's product work.
   - Tests that WILL pin it (forward reference — they land in atom 4 of this priority): name the planned suites, e.g. `packages/core/tests/directive.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`, and the daemon tests under `packages/daemon/tests/`.
   - State: mark this row explicitly as UNDER CONSTRUCTION / building for this priority — do NOT label it `aligned` or `already-fixed`, because the runtime does not exist yet (it lands in atoms 1–2–4). Use a clearly non-shipped state word and say the mechanism is being built now.

2) RECONCILE the existing `Deb tier-2 watcher and Oscar escalation` row (currently around line 57). It documents Oscar's `deb-investigate` escalation routing through the existing numbered directive file / fault-triage path and marks it `aligned`. Because this priority REMOVES the within-run `deb-investigate` directive in atom 2 (the daemon-resident dialogue replaces it), update that row so it no longer presents `deb-investigate` as the live proactive Oscar→Deb escalation path. Point the proactive, Oscar-initiated, post-wrap-capable repair to the new ADR-0036 dialogue entry from (1). PRESERVE the watcher + Oscar-only nudge half (full-run Deb watcher, `deb-status.json`, `deb-nudge.json`) — that ownership stays with the `deb-follows-oscar` priority and must remain documented. Do not leave the row internally contradicting the new contract; note that `deb-investigate` is being removed by this priority.

Also state plainly in the new content that no second repair lane and no second commit lane are created, and the dialogue does not touch the build directive loop.

Acceptance criteria:
- New repair-dialogue owner entry present with: source of truth, daemon-resident Bob-free trigger, propose→evaluate→direct handshake, founder tier, commit path (reuse ADR-0016 + ADR-0023), planned pinning tests, and a non-shipped/under-construction state.
- The `Deb tier-2 watcher and Oscar escalation` row no longer claims `deb-investigate` is the live Oscar→Deb escalation path; it reflects the pending removal and points to the new dialogue entry, while keeping the watcher/nudge ownership intact.
- Content explicitly affirms: no second orchestration lane, no second commit lane, no touch to the build directive loop.

What must NOT break:
- Edit ONLY `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md`. No other file.
- 'Just docs' can be behavior-pinned: run `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts` (plus any test that asserts this doc's content) and confirm it passes. Report the exact command and its exit code in your summary, and include the diff.

data.task:
DESIGN SLICE — atom 1 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop` (ADR-0036). DOCUMENTATION ONLY: produce ONE new design doc, `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`. Edit no code, tests, personas, or ADRs. This is the concrete build spec that atom 2 implements against; ADR-0036 deferred exactly these mechanism details to this build.

First READ the real anchors and cite them by file:line in the doc (do not invent APIs):
- `cocoder/decisions/0036-oscar-deb-repair-dialogue.md` (the decision of record) and `cocoder/decisions/0016-deb-scoped-repair-fallback.md` (repair authority, propose/repair verdict shape, §4 lightest-home escalation), `cocoder/decisions/0023-workspace-commit-spine.md` (the one commit spine).
- The idle Oz repair tool to MODEL THE TRIGGER ON: `requestOzRepair` in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` and its `repair` entry in `packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts`; note its idle-only guard and how it runs a headless turn then gate-commits (`runHeadlessThenGateCommit` / `commitScoped`).
- The existing in-run Deb repair/triage + commit path for reuse: `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts` (~868-898) and `packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts` (~574-584); `packages/personas/base/deb.md` repair posture.
- The post-wrap support-commit operation as the closest 'Oscar invokes a daemon op via a `cocoder oz …` command' precedent: `requestSupportCommitRun` in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`, its HTTP/oz-chat routes, and the `cocoder oz commit-support <runId>` CLI surface.
- `packages/core/src/runner/directive.ts` (so the design can state, concretely, that NO new within-`runRun` directive kind is added).
- The new owner row added in atom 0: `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` (the `Oscar-Deb autonomous repair dialogue` row) — the design doc must be consistent with it and cross-link to ADR-0036 as source of truth, NOT re-assert the invariants as a second authority.

The design doc must specify, concretely enough that atom 2 is buildable:
1. ENTRY / TRIGGER — how Oscar files a repair request. It must be a daemon-resident, Oscar-initiated, Bob-free standing capability (model on `requestOzRepair`), runnable any time INCLUDING after Oscar has wrapped, and NOT a within-`runRun` directive kind. Name the concrete entry surface (e.g. a new daemon operation + the `cocoder oz …` CLI command Oscar runs, analogous to `commit-support`), and the idle/rate-limit guards reused from the Oz-repair pattern.
2. HANDSHAKE ARTIFACTS — the propose->evaluate->direct flow. Define each artifact's JSON shape and its on-disk home (NOT `directive-n.json`): (a) Oscar's repair request (named problem + evidence), (b) Deb's response: either an applied in-scope fix verdict OR a proposal handed back, (c) Oscar's recorded evaluation + direction, (d) the founder-escalation record for risky items. Give field names and an example for each.
3. STATE MACHINE — the explicit states/transitions: requested -> (Deb applies in-scope fix | Deb proposes) -> [if proposal] Oscar evaluates -> directs -> (Deb applies | escalate to founder). Say where each transition is driven (daemon op vs Oscar/Deb headless turn) and how evidence is recorded.
4. COMMIT PATH — Deb's in-scope fixes commit through the EXISTING ADR-0016 repair path + ADR-0023 spine (`runCommitGate`, gate-enforced against Deb's active scope), out-of-scope held back and surfaced. Explicitly: no second commit lane.
5. FOUNDER TIER — how genuinely risky / hard-to-reverse items escalate one tier to the founder (ADR-0016 §4 lightest-home, extended by ADR-0036), and what artifact surfaces that.
6. NON-GOALS / INVARIANTS (state explicitly, cross-linked to ADR-0036): Oscar↔Deb only — Deb never directs Bob; never enters or touches the build directive loop; no second orchestration lane; never rescues a formally failed run; does not replace Oscar's per-atom verify gate.
7. BUILD MAP for atom 2: which existing modules/functions are EXTENDED vs what is NET-NEW (file-level), and which suites atom 4 will add to pin it (`packages/core/tests/directive.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`, daemon tests under `packages/daemon/tests/`). Also note that atom 2 removes the obsolete within-run `deb-investigate` lane (the `deb-investigate` kind in `directive.ts`, the `oscar-requested-deb-investigation` fail path in `runner.ts`, and its language in `prompts.ts`).

Acceptance criteria:
- The doc names a concrete daemon-resident entry surface, artifact JSON shapes with on-disk locations, the propose->evaluate->direct state machine, idle/rate-limit guards, the reused commit path, the founder tier, and the explicit non-goals.
- Every claim about an existing function/module cites a real file (file:line) that actually exists — no invented APIs.
- Nothing in the doc contradicts ADR-0036 or the atom-0 owner row; the doc points to ADR-0036 as source of truth and does not create a second contract authority.
- The design explicitly forbids a within-`runRun` directive kind and a second commit/orchestration lane.

What must NOT break:
- Create/edit ONLY `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`. No other file.
- Run `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts` and report the exact command + exit code (cheap safety check that no doc-pinning suite breaks). Include the new file's full content in your summary.

data.task:
REMOVE the obsolete within-run `deb-investigate` Oscar→Deb escalation lane (atom 2a of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036). This is the SUBTRACTIVE half only — do NOT build the new daemon dialogue here (that is the next atom). The lane is rejected by ADR-0036 because it tied 'ask Deb for help' to a formal run failure; it is being replaced by the daemon-resident dialogue. Update the directly-coupled tests IN THIS ATOM so the tree stays green (do not defer them).

I re-derived the complete LIVE site set with a fresh grep — touch exactly these 5 files and nothing else:

PRODUCTION:
1. `packages/core/src/runner/directive.ts`:
   - Remove the `{ kind: 'deb-investigate'; blocker: string }` member from the `Directive` union (~line 17).
   - Remove the `if (d.kind === 'deb-investigate') { … }` parse branch (~lines 82-87) so parsing a `deb-investigate` directive now FALLS THROUGH to the final throw.
   - Update the final error message (~line 95) to list only the remaining kinds: `directive: "kind" must be "delegate" or "wrapup"`.
2. `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`:
   - Remove the `if (directive.kind === 'deb-investigate') { return await fail('oscar-requested-deb-investigation', directive.blocker, null) }` branch (~lines 1349-1350). The general `triageFault`/fault path and all OTHER fault callers stay; only this Oscar-escalation branch goes.
3. `packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts`:
   - Remove the `{"kind": "deb-investigate", "blocker": …}` directive example block (~line 194) and the surrounding sentence that advertises it.
   - Remove the headless-turn `deb-investigate` instruction (~line 311).
   - Remove the `deb-investigate` clause from the NEXT-or-wrap dispatch text (~line 589) so the dispatch offers only `delegate` and `wrapup`. Keep the rest of that dispatch text intact.

COUPLED TESTS (update in this same atom — they go red the instant the kind is removed):
4. `packages/core/tests/directive.test.ts`:
   - Replace the test that asserts `deb-investigate` PARSES (~lines 34-35) with one asserting `parseDirective(JSON.stringify({ kind: 'deb-investigate', blocker: '…' }))` now THROWS (the kind is gone).
5. `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`:
   - Remove/​invert the prompt assertion that the Oscar prompt contains `"kind": "deb-investigate"` (~line 2034) — assert it is NOT present.
   - Remove the whole `oscar-requested-deb-investigation` fail-path test case (the one whose directives include `{ kind: 'deb-investigate', blocker }` ~line 2353 and which asserts the `oscar-requested-deb-investigation` event and `triage-dispatch` fault ~lines 2390-2391).

Acceptance criteria:
- `grep -rn "deb-investigate\|oscar-requested-deb-investigation" packages/core/src packages/core/tests` returns NOTHING except, if you keep an inverted assertion, the literal string inside a `.not.toContain(...)` / throws-expectation. No live union member, parse branch, fail branch, prompt clause, or passing parse/fail-path test remains.
- `delegate` and `wrapup` directives still parse and drive the loop unchanged.

What must NOT break (preserve intact):
- ADR-0016 reactive fault/triage/repair: the `triageFault` function and every OTHER fault caller stay. Only the Oscar `deb-investigate`→fail escalation branch is removed.
- The full-run Deb watcher, `deb-status.json`, and `deb-nudge.json` (Oscar-only nudge) behavior — do not touch.
- Do NOT edit historical/record files that legitimately mention `deb-investigate`: `cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md`, `cocoder/tickets/**`, `cocoder/decisions/**`, `cocoder/priorities/**` (incl. archive), `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`, and `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` (already reconciled in atom 0).

EVIDENCE (run and report exact commands + exit codes):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/directive.test.ts packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec tsc -p packages/core --noEmit` (or the repo's core typecheck) to prove the union/type removal compiles clean.
Include the full diff in your summary.

data.task:
Build the DATA LAYER + STATE MACHINE for the Oscar↔Deb repair dialogue, WITH unit tests (atom 3 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036). This is the first additive slice — NET-NEW only, pure/deterministic, fully tested. Do NOT build the daemon operation, headless turns, commit integration, HTTP route, oz-chat command, or CLI here (those are later atoms). Build strictly against the design doc `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md` (read it first; it is the build spec).

Create `packages/daemon/src/oscar-deb-repair.ts` containing ONLY pure data + state logic:

1. TYPES for each handshake artifact, matching the design's shapes exactly:
   - `OscarRepairRequest` (schemaVersion, dialogueId, workspaceId, sourceRunId?, requestedBy, createdAt, problem, evidence[], desiredOutcome?). Evidence item = { kind, ref, summary }.
   - `DebRepairResponse` as a discriminated union on `kind`: `'applied'` (disposition, mode, summary, diagnosis, whyCocoderOwned, filesChanged[], verification, remainingRisk, commit{sha,committedPaths[],outOfLanePaths[]}) | `'proposal'` (disposition, summary, diagnosis, recommendedChanges[], verificationPlan[], risk, needsFounder).
   - `OscarEvaluation` (evaluatedBy, createdAt, verdict ∈ {accept-applied, direct-deb-to-apply, revise, escalate-founder}, reason, direction?{action,scope[],verificationRequired[]}).
   - `FounderEscalation` (kind:'founder-escalation', createdAt, reason, lightestHome, options[], recommendedOption, evidenceRefs[]).
   - `DialogueState` union = the 11 states named in the design's State Machine section (requested, waiting-for-idle, deb-running, deb-applied, deb-proposed, oscar-evaluating, oscar-directed, deb-directed-running, founder-escalated, complete, failed).
   - An evidence-log entry type for `evidence.jsonl` ({ts, state, artifact, summary}).
2. VALIDATORS/PARSERS in the style of `packages/core/src/runner/directive.ts` `parseDirective` (parse JSON, throw a clear `Error` on malformed input): `parseOscarRepairRequest`, `parseDebRepairResponse`, `parseOscarEvaluation`, `parseFounderEscalation`. Enforce: non-empty trimmed `problem` capped at 4000 chars; at least one evidence item with non-empty `summary`; valid `kind`/`verdict`/`risk` discriminants; required fields present. Reject unknown/missing kinds with a message listing the allowed values.
3. `makeDialogueId(nowMs: number, token: string): string` → `repair-<nowMs>-<token>`. Inject clock + token as params — do NOT call `Date.now()`/`Math.random()` inside any exported function, so tests are deterministic.
4. PATH HELPERS: given workspaceId + dialogueId, return the on-disk artifact paths under `local/oz/<workspaceId>/repair-dialogues/<dialogueId>/` exactly as the design lists (request.json, deb-response.json, oscar-evaluation.json, founder-escalation.json, evidence.jsonl, deb-turn.log, oscar-turn.log). Centralize the base-dir construction in one helper.
5. PURE STATE MACHINE: `nextDialogueState(current: DialogueState, event): DialogueState` encoding the design's transition table exactly (requested→waiting-for-idle|deb-running; waiting-for-idle→deb-running; deb-running→deb-applied|deb-proposed; deb-proposed→oscar-evaluating; oscar-evaluating→oscar-directed; oscar-directed→deb-directed-running|founder-escalated; deb-directed-running→complete; deb-applied→complete; founder-escalated→complete; any active state→failed). Throw on an illegal transition.

UNIT TESTS in `packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts` proving: each validator accepts a well-formed artifact and rejects each malformed case (empty problem, over-length problem, zero evidence, evidence with empty summary, bad kind, bad verdict); `makeDialogueId` format; path helpers return the exact expected paths; and every legal transition succeeds while representative illegal transitions throw (including deb-applied vs deb-proposed branching, oscar-directed→deb-directed-running vs →founder-escalated, and any-active→failed).

Acceptance criteria:
- One new source file and one new test file, both under `packages/daemon/`. No existing file modified.
- Types/states/paths match `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`; validators mirror the throw-on-malformed style of `parseDirective`.
- No `Date.now()`/`Math.random()` inside exported functions (injected instead).

What must NOT break:
- NET-NEW ONLY: do not modify `launcher.ts`, `oz-chat.ts`, `routes.ts`, the runner, `directive.ts`, `prompts.ts`, personas, or any existing file. No daemon operation, no headless turn, no commit, no route, no CLI in this atom.
- This module is daemon-resident only — it introduces NO within-`runRun` directive kind and NO second commit lane (it does not commit at all yet).

EVIDENCE (report exact commands + exit codes, include full diff):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec tsc -p packages/daemon --noEmit`

data.detail: awaiting-directive atom 4: awaiting directive 4

data.task:
Implement the daemon-resident operation `requestOscarDebRepair` that drives the Oscar↔Deb repair dialogue (atom 4 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036), reusing the atom-3 data layer (`packages/daemon/src/oscar-deb-repair.ts`) and the EXISTING headless-repair + commit spine. ONE synchronous daemon operation drives the whole state machine (Oscar's evaluation is an autonomous headless turn — no human pause, so do NOT split it across calls or persist-and-resume). Build against `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`. Do NOT add entry surfaces (oz-chat/HTTP/CLI) or edit personas in this atom — those are later atoms.

Add `export async function requestOscarDebRepair(ctx: OzContext, input)` to `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`. input: { workspaceId, sourceRunId?, problem, evidence[], desiredOutcome?, requestedBy:'oscar' }.

MANDATORY behaviors:
1. IDLE GUARD — follow `requestSupportCommitRun` semantics (`packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:801-807`), NOT `requestOzRepair`'s blunt `ctx.inFlight.size > 0` (`:1007`). ADR-0036 REQUIRES this dialogue to run after Oscar has wrapped, so: refuse with 409 ONLY when the workspace has an ACTIVELY-RUNNING run — i.e. if `sourceRunId` is given, look it up and refuse if `run.status === 'running'`; also refuse if a DIFFERENT run id holds `ctx.inFlight.get(workspaceId)`. ALLOW the operation when the sourceRun is wrapped/terminal (status not 'running') or the workspace is idle. A test MUST prove the wrapped-sourceRun case proceeds.
2. VALIDATE input. Build the `OscarRepairRequest` and validate it with `parseOscarRepairRequest` (atom-3) — round-trip through the validators; on invalid input return 400 with the validator error. Non-empty problem ≤4000 chars, ≥1 evidence item with non-empty summary.
3. RESOLVE assignments: Deb's cli/model + Deb effective writeScope via `resolveEffectivePersona(sources, assignments, 'deb')` (mirror the Oscar resolution at `:812-821`); resolve Oscar's cli/model for the evaluation turn the same way. If Deb (or, when needed, Oscar) is unassigned/disabled return 409.
4. dialogueId via `makeDialogueId(Date.now(), <shortToken>)`; create the dialogue dir from `repairDialoguePaths`; write `request.json`; append an `evidence.jsonl` entry per transition; emit an Oz event (`emitOzEvent`) and `appendAudit` with the dialogueId. Use `nextDialogueState` (atom-3) to advance state and to reject illegal transitions.
5. DRIVE THE MACHINE (one operation):
   a. Run a Deb headless turn — build a Deb repair-dialogue prompt that hands Deb the request + the ADR-0036 constraints and instructs: an EASY, clearly in-scope CoCoder-machinery fix → edit the files AND emit an `applied` JSON on stdout; if UNSURE/risky → edit nothing and emit a `proposal` JSON. Run it through the SAME headless mechanism `runHeadlessThenGateCommit` uses (`ctx.getAdapter(cli).build(...)` + `ctx.runHeadless ?? runHeadlessProcess`), capturing `turn.output`. Parse `turn.output` with `parseDebRepairResponse`.
   b. `applied` → commit Deb's edits through `gateCommitRepair` (THE existing repair spine — Deb's writeScope, message `deb-repair`, a deb author), capture committed/sha/outOfLane, write `deb-response.json` including the commit object; advance deb-running→deb-applied→complete.
   c. `proposal` → spawn an Oscar headless EVALUATION turn (request + Deb proposal), parse `parseOscarEvaluation`. Then:
      - `direct-deb-to-apply` → run a SECOND Deb headless turn carrying Oscar's direction, commit via `gateCommitRepair`, write artifacts; advance to complete.
      - `escalate-founder` (or Deb's `needsFounder`/high risk) → write `founder-escalation.json` (ADR-0016 §4 lightest-home extended), emit event, return a founder-facing result; NO repair commit happens in this state.
      - `revise` → record the evaluation + reason and return without committing (single round; the founder/next request re-initiates).
   d. Any headless turn non-zero exit, malformed artifact, timeout, or commit error → transition to `failed`, record it in `evidence.jsonl`, return 500 with the detail. Nothing is committed on failure.
6. RETURN a `LaunchResult` reporting: final state, dialogueId, artifact paths, and committedPaths/commitSha/outOfLanePaths where a commit happened.

HARD INVARIANTS (a test must guard these):
- The operation NEVER spawns Bob/the builder and NEVER enters or calls `runRun` / the build directive loop. Assert no builder dispatch and no run-loop involvement.
- ONE commit lane only: all commits go through `gateCommitRepair`; do NOT add any other git commit path. Out-of-lane Deb paths are committed-and-flagged (surface `outOfLanePaths`) per ADR-0023, not a second lane.
- Deb never directs Bob; Oscar's per-atom verify gate over Bob's product work is untouched (do not modify it).
- Never rescues a formally failed run: a failed dialogue returns failure; it does not flip any run's status to success.

TESTS — add `packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts` using the existing daemon test harness (build a fake `OzContext` with `runHeadless` injected to return canned per-persona stdout, a fake `git`, an in-memory store — follow the patterns in `packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts` and `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts`). Prove:
- refuse (409) while a workspace run is actively `running`;
- ALLOW and proceed when the only run is the wrapped/terminal `sourceRunId` (post-wrap requirement);
- invalid input → 400;
- Deb `applied` → `gateCommitRepair` invoked, commit recorded, `deb-response.json` applied, state complete;
- Deb `proposal` → Oscar `direct-deb-to-apply` → second Deb turn → commit → complete;
- `escalate-founder` → `founder-escalation.json` written, NO repair commit;
- Deb turn non-zero exit → failed, nothing committed;
- the operation never spawns the builder and never calls `runRun`.

What must NOT break:
- Edit only `packages/daemon/**` (extend `launcher.ts`, add the test, optionally small new prompt-builder helpers). Do NOT touch the runner, `directive.ts`, `prompts.ts`, personas, routes, oz-chat, or CLI in this atom.
- Preserve all existing daemon behavior — `requestOzRepair`, `requestSupportCommitRun`, `requestAuthoringPlay`, and `runHeadlessThenGateCommit` stay intact.

EVIDENCE (report exact commands + exit codes; include the full diff):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/daemon` (prove no regression in the existing daemon suite, since launcher.ts is shared)
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec tsc -p packages/daemon --noEmit`

data.task:
Expose the `requestOscarDebRepair` daemon operation through its entry surfaces so Oscar can initiate the repair dialogue (atom 5 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036), including after wrap. These surfaces ONLY call the existing operation from atom 4 — do not change the operation's logic, and add no new commit path or directive kind. Build against `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md` (Entry/Trigger + Atom 2 Build Map) and mirror exactly how `requestSupportCommitRun` is exposed.

Wire THREE surfaces:

1. HTTP route in `packages/daemon/src/routes.ts`: add `POST /workspaces/:workspaceId/oscar-deb-repairs`. Mirror the existing run/workspace POST routes (read JSON body with the same `readJsonBody`/invalid-JSON→400 guard used by `teardown`/`/workspaces`). Map body `{ problem, evidence?, sourceRunId?, desiredOutcome? }` into `requestOscarDebRepair(ctx, { workspaceId, requestedBy: 'oscar', problem, evidence, sourceRunId?, desiredOutcome? })` and `sendJson(res, status, out)`. Import `requestOscarDebRepair` from `./launcher.js` alongside the existing imports.

2. oz-chat command in `packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts`: 
   - Add an `OzCommand` variant `{ kind: 'oscar-deb-repair'; problem: string; sourceRunId?: string }`.
   - In `parseOzCommand`, parse a `deb-repair <problem...>` verb with an optional `--run <runId>` flag (the problem is the remaining text). Empty problem → `unknownCommand()`.
   - Add `requestOscarDebRepair` to the `OzChatOps` interface and `defaultOps` (import as e.g. `oscarDebRepairOp`), exactly like `repairOz`/`supportCommitRun` are wired.
   - In `executeOzCommand`, handle `command.kind === 'oscar-deb-repair'`: it needs a `workspaceId` (return the same no-workspace guard the `repair` command uses). Build a request that passes the operation's validation — synthesize a single evidence item from the problem when none is available (e.g. `[{ kind: 'oz-chat', ref: 'oz-chat', summary: <problem> }]`), and dispatch through `ops.requestOscarDebRepair(ctx, { workspaceId, requestedBy: 'oscar', problem, evidence, ...(sourceRunId ? { sourceRunId } : {}) })`.
   - Add a reply formatter (mirroring `repairReply`/`supportCommitReply`) that summarizes the outcome (applied / proposed→directed-applied / founder-escalated / failed) and surfaces committedPaths/commitSha/outOfLanePaths when present, including the same 'out-of-lane committed and flagged, NOT withheld' phrasing `repairReply` uses.
   - Update `HELP_HINT`, the `OzCommand['kind']`/executable-command unions, and the `type`/`command` action unions to include the new command so types stay exhaustive.

3. CLI in `packages/cli/src/run.ts` + `packages/cli/src/client.ts`: add `cocoder oz request-deb-repair <workspaceId> --problem <text> [--run <runId>] [--evidence <json>]`, mirroring the existing `cocoder oz commit-support <runId>` command and its client method. Parse `--problem` (required; error to usage if missing), optional `--run`, optional `--evidence` (a JSON array of {kind,ref,summary}); when `--evidence` is omitted, synthesize one evidence item from the problem so the request validates. The client posts to `POST /workspaces/:workspaceId/oscar-deb-repairs` and prints the JSON result. Add the new command to the CLI `usage` string.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each surface ends up calling `requestOscarDebRepair` with a request that passes its validation (non-empty problem ≤4000, ≥1 evidence item with non-empty summary), and surfaces its status/body back.
- The oz-chat and CLI surfaces are usable without the caller hand-constructing evidence (synthesized default), while still allowing structured evidence via the HTTP body / `--evidence`.
- No change to `requestOscarDebRepair`'s logic, no new commit lane, no within-`runRun` directive kind.

TESTS (mirror existing harness patterns):
- `packages/daemon/tests/` (the route/mutations suite, e.g. `mutations.test.ts`): the `POST /workspaces/:id/oscar-deb-repairs` route invokes the operation and returns its result; invalid JSON body → 400.
- `packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts`: `parseOzCommand('deb-repair <problem> --run <id>')` parses correctly; `executeOzCommand` dispatches to an injected `requestOscarDebRepair` op (assert it is called with requestedBy 'oscar', the problem, synthesized evidence, and the sourceRunId when --run given) and renders the reply; the no-workspace guard fires like `repair`.
- `packages/cli/tests/client.test.ts`: the `request-deb-repair` client method POSTs to the right path with the right body and surfaces the response; `--problem` missing → usage error.

What must NOT break:
- Edit only `packages/daemon/**` and `packages/cli/**`. Do NOT touch the runner, personas, `directive.ts`, `prompts.ts`, or the atom-4 operation body.
- Preserve all existing routes, oz-chat commands, and CLI commands intact (run the full daemon suite to confirm).

EVIDENCE (report exact commands + exit codes; include full diff):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/cli`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec tsc -p packages/daemon --noEmit` and `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec tsc -p packages/cli --noEmit`

Write scope: packages/daemon/**, packages/cli/**

data.task:
PERSONA/PROMPT ALIGNMENT for the Oscar↔Deb autonomous repair dialogue (atom 6 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036). Teach the Oscar and Deb BASE personas to initiate and service the dialogue and the founder tier, keep it ADR-0012-portable (role-generic), and pin the new language with focused test assertions. This is Surface-A base governance under `packages/personas/base/**`.

ADD (do not remove or reword any existing pinned phrase — the base-personas test asserts several `toContain` strings verbatim):

1. `packages/personas/base/oscar.md` — add a short subsection (e.g. under 'How you work' or near the post-wrap support paragraph) teaching that Oscar can PROACTIVELY initiate a machinery-repair dialogue with Deb, decoupled from the Bob build loop:
   - When Oscar identifies a real orchestration/machinery issue — including AFTER he has wrapped — he tasks Deb to research and propose a fix through the standing, daemon-resident repair-dialogue capability (e.g. `cocoder oz request-deb-repair <workspaceId> --problem <text>`), NOT a within-run directive and NOT the Bob build loop.
   - Deb either applies an easy, clearly in-scope fix herself, or hands a proposal back for Oscar to evaluate and direct — the propose→evaluate→direct handshake. Oscar records his evaluation/direction.
   - Genuinely risky or hard-to-reverse machinery changes escalate one tier further, to the founder (ADR-0016 §4 lightest-home, extended by ADR-0036).
   - Invariants to state: this is Oscar↔Deb only and never involves or directs Bob; it never enters the build directive loop; it never rescues a formally failed run; it reuses the existing Deb repair authority and the one commit spine (no second commit lane); and it does not replace Oscar's per-atom verify gate over Bob's product work. Cite ADR-0036.

2. `packages/personas/base/deb.md` — add a short subsection teaching that Deb SERVICES Oscar-initiated repair requests as a distinct, PROACTIVE entry into her existing repair authority (separate from the reactive fault-triage the runner dispatches):
   - On an Oscar repair request, Deb researches and either applies an easy, clearly in-scope CoCoder-machinery fix (landed as a `deb-repair` through the existing commit spine) or, when unsure/risky, returns a proposal for Oscar to evaluate and direct.
   - If Oscar directs apply, Deb applies under that direction; genuinely risky items escalate to the founder.
   - This can fire any time, INCLUDING after Oscar has wrapped, and is Bob-free. Reaffirm the existing invariants already in deb.md: Deb never directs Bob, and a repair is never a run rescue. Cite ADR-0036.
   - Keep it consistent with the existing 'What you do'/'Repair'/'Repair evidence' sections; do not duplicate or contradict them.

PORTABILITY (ADR-0012): keep both additions role-generic — teach the behavior of the orchestrator and the repair engineer (initiate machinery repair; fix-or-propose; risk → founder), using the product-level nouns the base personas already carry (ADR references and `cocoder oz …` commands are already present in these files and are allowed; the shared-standards ADR ban does NOT apply to persona files). Do not embed workspace-specific priority names or run numbers.

PIN THE NEW LANGUAGE: add focused assertions to `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts` (new `test(...)` cases, mirroring the existing Deb/Oscar `toContain` style) that pin the new Oscar-initiates and Deb-services dialogue language and the founder tier, so the governance is enforced and cannot silently drift.

Acceptance criteria:
- Oscar persona teaches proactive, post-wrap-capable initiation via the daemon repair-dialogue capability, the propose→evaluate→direct handshake, the founder tier, and the Bob-free / no-second-lane / no-run-rescue / verify-gate-untouched invariants, citing ADR-0036.
- Deb persona teaches servicing the Oscar-initiated request (fix-or-propose), Oscar direction, founder escalation, post-wrap/Bob-free, citing ADR-0036, consistent with her existing repair sections.
- New base-personas test assertions pin both additions.
- Every pre-existing pinned phrase in deb.md/oscar.md is preserved verbatim.

What must NOT break:
- Edit only `packages/personas/**` (the two base persona files + the base-personas test). Do NOT touch the runner, daemon, CLI, directive.ts, prompts.ts, or other personas.
- Do not remove/alter existing `toContain`-pinned phrases or the frontmatter/writeScope of either persona.

EVIDENCE (report exact commands + exit codes; include full diff):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts`
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/personas-propagation.test.ts packages/core/tests/prompts.test.ts` (prove persona propagation/prompt assembly still passes)

Write scope: packages/personas/**

data.task:
FINAL CLOSEOUT: owner-map flip + structural guard + runnable proof harness (atom 7 of `deb-oscar-repair-loop`, ADR-0036). The runtime now exists end-to-end (data layer, operation, surfaces, personas) and the deb-investigate lane is removed. Do THREE things:

1. OWNER-MAP FLIP — `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md`:
   - Flip the `Oscar-Deb autonomous repair dialogue` row from `under-construction` to `aligned`, replacing the 'planned/will pin' language with the REAL shipped citations now in HEAD: source of truth ADR-0036 + `docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md`; data layer `packages/daemon/src/oscar-deb-repair.ts`; operation `requestOscarDebRepair` in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`; surfaces `POST /workspaces/:id/oscar-deb-repairs` (`packages/daemon/src/routes.ts`), the `deb-repair` oz-chat command (`packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts`), and `cocoder oz request-deb-repair` (`packages/cli/src/run.ts` + `client.ts`); commit path `gateCommitRepair` (the one spine, no second lane); persona alignment in `packages/personas/base/oscar.md` / `deb.md`; tests `packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/tests/oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts`, `packages/cli/tests/client.test.ts`, `packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts`, and `packages/core/tests/directive.test.ts` (deb-investigate now rejected).
   - Update the `Deb tier-2 watcher and Oscar-only nudge` row: DROP the stale 'pending removal' language — the within-run `deb-investigate` directive IS removed now; state it as done and keep pointing proactive repair at the dialogue row.
   - Keep ONE map — extend the existing Owner Inventory / pipeline tables; do not spawn a parallel map.

2. STRUCTURAL GUARD — extend the single existing enforcer `packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts` (do NOT create a parallel enforcer file) with a test that locks 'no second orchestration lane in the build loop': assert `parseDirective` rejects `{kind:'deb-investigate',...}` (throws), that the build-loop directive kinds are exactly delegate and wrapup, and that `packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts` contains no `deb-investigate` string. This fails if the rejected within-run lane ever reappears.

3. PROOF HARNESS — `scripts/proof-oscar-deb-repair.mjs`, a one-command founder proof modeled on `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` (shell out via execFile; `node scripts/proof-oscar-deb-repair.mjs`). It maps each objective 'Verified when' clause to the suite that proves it and asserts each is green, printing a clause→evidence PASS map and exiting nonzero if any fails. Cover: (a) Oscar initiates post-wrap and the op routes to Deb without Bob/`runRun` (oscar-deb-repair-op.test.ts: wrapped-source-run-allowed + never-spawns-Bob/empty-listRuns); (b) Deb lands an in-scope fix via the existing spine OR returns a proposal Oscar evaluates and directs (op test: applied-commit + proposal→direct→apply); (c) risky items escalate to the founder with no commit (op test: founder-escalation); (d) the dialogue never rescues a failed dialogue / commits nothing on failure (op test: failed-turn); (e) the build loop carries no second lane (orchestration-contracts.test.ts guard + directive.test.ts deb-investigate rejection). Use the actual test names/files; do not assert clauses no suite covers.

Acceptance criteria:
- The repair-dialogue owner row reads `aligned` with real file citations; the watcher row no longer says removal is pending.
- The new orchestration-contracts guard fails if `deb-investigate` returns to the directive schema or prompts.
- `node scripts/proof-oscar-deb-repair.mjs` exits 0 on the clean tree and prints a clause→evidence PASS map.

What must NOT break:
- Edit only `docs/**`, `packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts`, and `scripts/**`. Do NOT modify runtime source (the operation, surfaces, personas, directive.ts, prompts.ts) — this atom is proof + docs only.
- 'Just docs/governance can be behavior-pinned': run the orchestration-contracts suite after editing the doc + guard.

EVIDENCE (report exact commands + exit codes; include full diff):
- `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts`
- `node scripts/proof-oscar-deb-repair.mjs` (must exit 0)

Write scope: docs/**, packages/core/tests/**, scripts/**
